pages.raw_text:
There is possibility that the
fight for control of the city park
system may be the issue in the
coming municipal campaign. The
proposed ordinance of the 100000
club has stirred up all manner of
opposition and comment. This Or
dinance proposes placing the man
agement of the parks in the hands
of board of 10 appointed by the
10000 club, this boardto appoint
successors upon the expiration of
terms of members.


Attorney F. T. Post, on behalf Of
the club, takes the position that the
council was compelled to submit
the proposed amendment to the
charter to a vote of the people, as
10 per cent of the voters at the last
election had signed a petition ask
ing that the council take such &C.
ton.


1t is stated in political circles
that the democrats would welcome
a campaign in which the turning
over of the parks to board as pro-
posed would be the issue. They are
confident the public would not sus-
tain law to take control of the
parks out Of it's hands and placing
this power in the hands of board
over which the public had absolute
ly no control.


City Attorney Geraghty in an in.
terview today took issue with Mr..
Post. He said


"Mr. Post lodges the real issue.
He does not say the amendment is
legal. AS lawyer he would not go
on record as saying that a charter
amendment OF this kind could en-
dow the park board with corpor-
ate entity and confer on 1t power Of
eminent domain, as this amend
ment would do. In addition it at
tempts to procribe procedure in
courts. What Mr.. Post means is
that regardless of whether the Or
dinance is good or bad the coun-
Gil can be compelled by the asso-
Citations behind it to submit 1t to
a vote of the people. | will do
these people the justice to assume
that they would not put the city,
through pride of opinion, to the
useless expense of submitting the
ordinance to vote of the people,
knowing that the proposed meas
ure is unconstitutional.


aside from the legal objections
to the ordinance 1t goes on the as
sumption that the people are too
ignorant to manage their own af-
fairs and must be put under a
guardianship of a self constituted,
self perpetuating board This idea
is contrary to the spirit of the
times and an amendment of this
kind is out of place when the peo-
pie are clamoring for a greater
share in their government and are
adopting the direct primary and
initiative and referendum and de,
mand the election of United States
senators by direct vote.


The general public does not
realize how easy it is to alter the
city charter and should consider
carefully every proposed amend
ment.


Pursuant to instructions from
the city council | will draft al
initiative and referendum ordin
ance that will permit the people Il
not only control the park system
but the entire city government al
we'll"
